_meta.description: Single source of truth for what a 'good' erg stroke looks like. Shared by Tier 1 (PWA, imported as JSON) and Tier 2 (Python config). All values are PROVISIONAL starting points from standard Concept2/coaching guidance and MUST be tuned against real side-view clips.
_meta.grading: For each metric: a value inside [target_min, target_max] grades 'good'. Outside target but within minor_tolerance grades 'minor'. Beyond that grades 'needs_work'. Angle metrics are degrees; timing metrics are milliseconds (negative = the event happens too early); ratio/cv metrics are unitless.

catch_shin_angle.label: Catch shin angle
catch_shin_angle.describe: Shin (knee->ankle) deviation from vertical at the catch. ~0 deg = vertical shins (ideal).

catch_back_angle.label: Catch back angle
catch_back_angle.describe: Torso (hip->shoulder) forward lean from vertical at the catch. Pivot from the hips, flat back; ~30-45 deg forward is the target window.
catch_back_angle._note: Weak discriminator: forward lean is common to good hinge AND bad over-reach. Cannot see lumbar rounding (BlazePose models torso as a straight line). Kept informational with a wide range; do not over-trust.
catch_back_angle.cue_low: lean forward more from the hips

legs_back_sequence.label: Legs-before-back sequencing
legs_back_sequence.describe: Offset between when the legs reach 50% of their drive extension and when the back reaches 50% of its swing-open. Positive = legs lead (good). Negative/near-zero = back opens too early (the classic strong-legs fault). Validated: good rowers ~+90-120ms, flawed ~-22 to 0ms.
legs_back_sequence.cue_high: let the back swing through sooner

arm_break_timing.label: Arm-break timing
arm_break_timing.describe: Offset between legs reaching 50% extension and elbows reaching 50% of their bend. Positive = arms break after legs (good). NOTE: not yet reliably validated on test clips; kept wide and low-priority to avoid false diagnoses until calibrated on continuous footage.
arm_break_timing.cue_high: finish the stroke with the arms

finish_layback_angle.label: Finish layback angle
finish_layback_angle.describe: Torso lean PAST vertical at the finish. Some layback is good for a strong finish; too much wastes energy and stresses the back.
finish_layback_angle._note: Softened from elite (30-45) to recreational ranges; validated good recreational rower laid back ~17deg, excessive demo ~52deg.
finish_layback_angle.cue_low: lean back a touch more at the finish

drive_recovery_ratio.label: Drive:recovery ratio
drive_recovery_ratio.describe: Recovery duration divided by drive duration. Drive is explosive, recovery is patient; ~1:2 (value ~2.0) is the classic target. Low value = rushing the slide.
drive_recovery_ratio._note: Softened floor from 1.8 to 1.5: validated competitive steady-state rower ran ~1.6. The idealized 1:2 is aspirational; real low-rate steady state is ~1.5-1.7.
drive_recovery_ratio.cue_high: a little quicker on the recovery

stroke_rate.label: Stroke rate (SPM)
stroke_rate.describe: Strokes per minute. Informational for steady-state low-impact cardio; a wide range is fine. Not strongly graded.

rhythm_consistency.label: Rhythm consistency
rhythm_consistency.describe: Coefficient of variation of stroke duration across the piece (stdev/mean). Lower = smoother, more repeatable strokes.
